part.text: I detect investigation/review intent - you asked for a read-only correctness review, not implementation. My approach: parallel codebase exploration + external reference check for implicit/BM25/EASE conventions, then synthesize PASS/FAIL with blockers and next experiment.

input.prompt:
1. TASK: Read-only review of the Kaggle RecSys Steam scoring workflow for data leakage, label leakage, evaluation mistakes, and candidate CSV/submission-format risks.
2. EXPECTED OUTCOME: Concise findings with file paths/line references where possible; classify each finding as blocking or non-blocking. Specifically verify per-user top-half evaluation, Label handling, candidate ID/user/game columns, and no-submit constraints.
3. REQUIRED TOOLS: Read/search tools only. Do not edit files. Do not run Kaggle submit. Do not write outputs.
4. MUST DO: Inspect scripts under scripts/ and relevant artifacts under artifacts/scores if needed. Focus on score_popularity_itemknn_ease.py, score_bpr_als.py, blend_score_files.py, recsys_played_utils.py, and the provided blend_evaluation_summary.json. Check for train/validation contamination, using Label in scoring, wrong top-half k selection, unstable tie breaking, malformed prediction CSV risks, and any code path that could submit to Kaggle.
5. MUST NOT DO: Do not modify files, do not run long experiments, do not call Kaggle, do not assume correctness without line evidence.
6. CONTEXT: User wants a concise PASS/FAIL correctness review for a no-submit workflow. Current working directory: /opt/data/kaggle/kmu-rec-sys-26-steam. Attached files are the four scripts and one validation blend JSON.

state.title: Review leakage risks

input.prompt:
1. TASK: Read-only review of model-scoring code for bugs in BM25/TFIDF itemKNN, EASE weighting, implicit ALS/BPR scoring, and z/rank/RRF blends.
2. EXPECTED OUTCOME: Concise findings with file paths/line references; classify blocking vs non-blocking. Verify math conventions, matrix orientation, score extraction, normalization, and blend construction.
3. REQUIRED TOOLS: Read/search tools only. Do not edit files. Do not run Kaggle submit. Do not run expensive training unless absolutely necessary; prefer static review.
4. MUST DO: Inspect score_popularity_itemknn_ease.py, score_bpr_als.py, blend_score_files.py, recsys_played_utils.py. Check BM25 IDF clipping, TFIDF weighting, item similarity dense conversion, EASE formula/sign/weighted input behavior, implicit ALS/BPR expected matrix shape and factor orientation, latent score extraction, and rank/RRF behavior.
5. MUST NOT DO: Do not modify files, do not submit, do not write artifacts, do not overfocus on performance unless it affects correctness.
6. CONTEXT: User wants no-submit correctness review of candidate scoring scripts for Kaggle RecSys Steam.

state.title: Review model bugs

input.prompt:
1. TASK: External reference check for correctness conventions relevant to implicit ALS/BPR, EASE, BM25/TFIDF itemKNN, and reciprocal-rank/z-score blending in recommender candidate scoring.
2. EXPECTED OUTCOME: Concise reference-backed notes on common pitfalls: implicit library matrix orientation and factor arrays, EASE closed-form formula/sign, BM25 weighting for implicit matrices, and RRF/rank blend interpretation. Include only facts that affect this code review.
3. REQUIRED TOOLS: Official docs, GitHub examples, and web/doc search. Read-only.
4. MUST DO: Verify whether implicit.als.AlternatingLeastSquares.fit and implicit.bpr.BayesianPersonalizedRanking.fit expect user-items vs item-users and what user_factors/item_factors mean. Verify EASE formula B = -P / diag(P) with diagonal zero. Verify BM25 common IDF handling for collaborative filtering.
5. MUST NOT DO: Do not inspect or modify local files; do not run code; do not return generic recommender explanations.
6. CONTEXT: Local code uses implicit model.fit(X) where X is user-item, then scores user_factors[ui] @ item_factors[gi]; EASE code computes P=inv(G+lambdaI), denom=-diag(P), B=P/denom[None,:].
